question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 unilaterally adds interface-level OSPF MD5 authentication on
GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R2↔R3 link, Area 0). R3 has no MD5
configured on its end. OSPF Hellos from R2 carry authentication; R3
drops them as unauthenticated → R3 stops seeing R2 entirely. R2 keeps
sending authenticated hellos which R3 cannot validate. Adjacency
drops to Down. The iBGP loopback session over OSPF cascades to drop.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2), interface-level OSPF MD5 authentication (authentication message-digest + key cisco123) was added unilaterally to GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R2<->R3 link, area 0) while R3 has none.
The two ends disagree on the OSPF authentication configuration for that link: R2's authenticated Hellos fail R3's (unconfigured) validation and R3's unauthenticated Hellos fail R2's check, so neither side accepts the other's Hellos and the OSPF adjacency on the R2<->R3 link cannot be maintained. Because R3 is the OSPF transit that resolves R4's loopback, the IGP route to that loopback is lost, the iBGP loopback session over it cannot stay up, and BGP next-hops can no longer be resolved.
Fix: remove the OSPF message-digest authentication and key from R2's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0.
expected_symptoms_summary:
OSPF adjacency state change (%ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG):
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "dead timer expired".
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "dead timer expired".
- Bilateral: both ends (R2, R3) report the transition.

%ROUTING-RIB-6-SERVER_ROUTING_DEPTH reported by R2, R4 (2 instances):
- R2: Recursion loop looking up prefix 192.0.2.4/32 in Vrf: "default" Tbl: "default" Safi: "Unicast" added by bgp
- R4: Recursion loop looking up prefix 192.0.2.2/32 in Vrf: "default" Tbl: "default" Safi: "Unicast" added by bgp

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 authentication message-digest
 message-digest-key 1 md5 cisco123

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 no authentication message-digest
 no message-digest-key 1

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:09:21.439 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: dead timer expired, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000

RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:09:19.439 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: dead timer expired, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:09:22.691 UTC: rib_mgr[1155]: %ROUTING-RIB-6-SERVER_ROUTING_DEPTH : Recursion loop looking up prefix 192.0.2.4/32 in Vrf: "default" Tbl: "default" Safi: "Unicast" added by bgp
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:09:20.543 UTC: rib_mgr[1155]: %ROUTING-RIB-6-SERVER_ROUTING_DEPTH : Recursion loop looking up prefix 192.0.2.2/32 in Vrf: "default" Tbl: "default" Safi: "Unicast" added by bgp
